intro: Heterosamara wattersii, commonly known as Watters' gentian, is a plant species in the gentian family that is native to Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, as well as potential antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Bioactive constituents of Heterosamara wattersii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and iridoids, which have been identified as contributing to its medicinal properties. The plant is typically used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, as well as in Western herbal medicine, where it may be employed to support immune function and reduce inflammation.
